plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-229 RP06.P08.M04.S13-RP06.P08.M06.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-229 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP06.P08.M04 secondary workflow tail, the full M05 permission/audit binding Hermes-receipt cycle, and the M06 synthetic fixture head (40 rows across three sections) with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-230 handoff, without opening Hermes runtime receipt emission, command runtime execution, DMS runtime dispatch, review/approval route runtime, state writes, idempotency persistence, workflow persistence, object storage, OCR, search, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-229 is a descriptor-only DMS-core Hermes binding-slice pack (RP06.P08.M04.S13-RP06.P08.M06.S08, risk B, 40 units) and passes read-only review with no findings. Structural verification confirms internal consistency across all artifacts: the registry binding (registry.js:548-563), requirements block (registry.js:3568-3622), contract current_pack (dms-core-contract.json:3-18), plan pack (closeout-pack-plan.json:413+), and baseline all agree on 40 units, range, risk B, upstream CP00-228, and next CP00-230. Deliverable counts reconcile exactly to 40 (implementation 18, hermes_evidence 19, test 2, claude_review 1) and micro-phase rows reconcile (M04=10, M05=22, M06=8). All 7 required public exports exist (service.js:5331/5364; validators.js:6945/6980/7043/7064/7087), are re-exported through index.js, and are exercised by tests (model.test.js:2663-2709). Boundary integrity is sound: every case-set row is descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / raw_payload_included=false; the CP229 functions are pure object constructors with no I/O, DB, network, or runtime dispatch; freezeCp229Result/freezeCp229Validation stamp a complete no-write attestation chain (registry.js:980-984 -> CP228 -> CP227 -> CP198 base) with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, so Hermes is used only for the deterministic review-packet/receipt pattern, not product runtime. Authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_final_approval_claimed=false, human_approval_route_required_before_runtime=true, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; the pack truthfully claims production_ready=false (status implemented_pending_review), so there is no overclaim and no weakening of production gates. CP178+ review hardening is in place: forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:1363-1372) rejects auth failures, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON with prose, tool-call-shaped output, and final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims; the readiness auth-preflight passed with 1446 non-zero stdout bytes and valid JSON. Pre-review validation gates are recorded passing in command-evidence.json, with closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred as post-review-gated. As a read-only reviewer I cannot re-execute the validation commands, but the static artifacts those commands check are fully consistent. This review does not constitute final approval; human final approval remains required before any runtime opening.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-229 dms core hermes binding slice descriptor pack
